blocks.text: The crank sensor consists of a pulsar rotor and a semiconductor that detects the rotor position. When the engine starts, the pulsar rotor rotates and the magnetic flux of the crank sensor fluctuates. The magnetic flux variation is converted into a pulse signal and sent to the PGM-FI ECU. The crank sensor is used to control the ignition timing and fuel injection timing of each cylinder. If the number of abnormal pulse signals from the crank sensor is detected more than a predetermined number of times, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

blocks.text: Pulsar If the pulse signal of the crank sensor detected when the rotor makes one revolution is other than 58 times 30 times or more.

items.text: Noise in the code (CRKN line signal) between PGM-FI ECU and crank sensor

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
